# Astropress

Astropress is a web application development framework for editorial, informational, and content-driven sites built on Astro with TypeScript and a Rust CLI.

## Key concepts

- **App Host**: where the Astro app runs (Cloudflare Pages, Vercel, Netlify, Render, GitHub Pages)
- **Content Services**: where content, media, auth, and sessions are stored (Cloudflare D1/R2/KV, Supabase, Runway, SQLite)
- **local-runtime-modules**: Vite alias seam — the host app provides the DB/auth implementation; the package provides the admin UI and route handlers
- **Admin panel**: injected routes under `/ap-admin/` — login, post editor, media, settings, users, redirects, comments, API tokens, webhooks
- **Security middleware**: area-aware CSP (`public` / `admin` / `auth` / `api`), CSRF via origin checking, `__Host-` cookies, rate limiting

## REST API

- Base URL: `/ap-api/v1/`
- Auth: `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — create tokens at `/ap-admin/api-tokens`
- OpenAPI 3.1 spec: `GET /ap-api/v1/openapi.json`
- Scopes: `content:read`, `content:write`, `media:read`, `media:write`, `settings:read`, `webhooks:manage`

### Endpoints

| Method | Path | Scope |
|--------|------|-------|
| GET | `/ap-api/v1/content` | content:read |
| POST | `/ap-api/v1/content` | content:write |
| GET | `/ap-api/v1/content/{id}` | content:read |
| PUT | `/ap-api/v1/content/{id}` | content:write |
| DELETE | `/ap-api/v1/content/{id}` | content:write |
| GET | `/ap-api/v1/media` | media:read |
| POST | `/ap-api/v1/media` | media:write |
| DELETE | `/ap-api/v1/media/{id}` | media:write |
| GET | `/ap-api/v1/revisions/{recordId}` | content:read |
| GET | `/ap-api/v1/settings` | settings:read |
| GET POST | `/ap-api/v1/webhooks` | webhooks:manage |

## Health check

`GET /ap/health` — no auth required, returns `{ status, version, uptime, timestamp }`

## MCP server

Package: `astropress-mcp`
Run: `npx astropress-mcp` (stdio transport)

Tools:
- `list_content(type?, status?)` — list content records
- `get_content(id)` — fetch single record by ID or slug
- `create_content(type, title, body, status)` — create a new content record
- `update_content(id, fields)` — update an existing record
- `list_media()` — list media assets
- `get_site_settings()` — fetch site settings
- `get_health()` — get health status

## CLI

```bash
astropress new <name>              # scaffold a new project
astropress dev                     # start local dev server
astropress doctor                  # check project health (human-readable)
astropress doctor --json           # machine-readable JSON health check
astropress doctor --strict         # exit 1 if any warnings
astropress import wordpress --source <xml> --artifact-dir <dir>
astropress services verify         # verify Content Services connectivity
astropress backup --output <dir>   # export SQLite snapshot
astropress restore --from <dir>    # restore from snapshot
```

## Core export paths

```ts
import { astropressIntegration } from "astropress/integration"         // astro.config.mjs
import { selectAstropressAdapter } from "astropress/adapters/project"  // local-runtime-modules.ts
import { astropressSecurityMiddleware } from "astropress/security"     // middleware.ts
import { registerCms } from "astropress"                               // astropress.config.ts
import { resolveProjectEnv } from "astropress/project-env"             // env inspection
import { AstropressProviderContract } from "astropress/platform-contracts" // custom adapters
import { handleHealthRequest } from "astropress/runtime-health"        // /ap/health handler
import { createLogger } from "astropress/runtime-logger"               // structured logging
```

## Content record shape

```ts
interface ContentRecord {
  id: string;          // same as slug
  slug: string;
  kind: "post" | "page";
  title: string;
  status: "draft" | "review" | "published" | "archived";
  body: string | null;
  metadata: {
    seoTitle: string;
    metaDescription: string;
    updatedAt: string;
  };
}
```

## Quick start

```bash
npx astropress new my-site
cd my-site
bun install
bun dev
# Admin panel available at http://localhost:4321/ap-admin
```
